name: Tetris Tango
coopSpin: Player 1 controls piece movement (left/right/drop) while Player 2 controls rotation. Both must coordinate in real-time to place pieces efficiently. Neither player can succeed alone.
winRules: Survive as long as possible. The round ends when the stack reaches the top. High score is based on lines cleared together.
moveRules: Player 1: Arrow keys for left, right, soft drop, hard drop. Player 2: Z/X keys for rotate clockwise/counter-clockwise. Pieces fall continuously.
setupInstructions: Standard 10x20 Tetris grid, empty at start. Random piece queue shared between both players. Piece preview shows next 3 pieces.
artStyleNotes: Neon synthwave palette with pink/cyan/purple gradients. Chunky pixel blocks with subtle glow effects. 16x16 tile size. Pulsing background grid lines.

name: Snake Charmer
coopSpin: Player 1 controls the snake's movement. Player 2 is the 'charmer' who places food pellets on the grid and can temporarily create safe walls to guide the snake. Both share a single snake and must communicate.
winRules: Reach target length (e.g., 50 segments) to win. Lose if the snake hits its own tail or the boundary. Score based on speed of completion.
moveRules: Player 1: WASD to change snake direction. Player 2: Mouse click to place food (limited supply, regenerates slowly), hold Shift+click to place temporary barrier (3 second duration, 10 second cooldown).
setupInstructions: 20x20 grid with snake starting at length 3 in the center. Player 2 starts with 3 food items. No initial obstacles.
artStyleNotes: Garden/nature theme with earthy greens and browns. Snake is a friendly cartoon serpent. Food appears as glowing fruits. Soft pixel art style, 8x8 tiles.

name: Pong Volley
coopSpin: Both players are on the same side, controlling two paddles stacked vertically. They must volley the ball against an AI wall that returns shots unpredictably. Players must cover for each other's gaps.
winRules: Keep a rally going to build a combo multiplier. Every 10 successful returns scores points. Missing the ball ends the rally and resets the multiplier. Reach 1000 points to win.
moveRules: Player 1: W/S keys to move upper paddle. Player 2: Up/Down arrows to move lower paddle. Paddles cannot overlap but can touch. Ball speeds up gradually during rallies.
setupInstructions: Players' paddles on left side of screen (top and bottom halves). AI wall on right side with slight randomness in return angles. Ball starts slow, launches toward AI wall.
artStyleNotes: Retro arcade black background with white/green phosphor CRT aesthetic. Scanline overlay. Paddles and ball are simple rectangles with slight bloom effect.

name: Word Bridge
coopSpin: Players alternate guessing letters to solve a word, but each player can only see HALF the revealed letters (odd positions vs even positions). They must verbally share clues without saying the actual letters they see.
winRules: Solve the word before 8 wrong guesses. Bonus points for fewer mistakes. Three words per round, total score determines success.
moveRules: Active player types a letter guess. After guessing, players swap. Each player's screen only shows letters in their assigned positions; other positions show '?' even if solved.
setupInstructions: Random word selected (6-10 letters). Player 1 assigned odd positions, Player 2 assigned even positions. 8 lives represented by bridge planks.
artStyleNotes: Cozy illustration style with a wooden rope bridge over a chasm. Each wrong guess removes a plank. Warm sunset oranges and soft shadows. Hand-drawn letter tiles.

name: Pong: Beach Volleyball
coopSpin: Two players share the entire near court of a Pong-style arena but play by beach volleyball rules: exactly 3 hits per side before the ball must cross the net. On hits 1 and 2 the ball auto-arcs toward whichever teammate did NOT just touch it, forcing the bump-set rhythm; on the team's third touch it auto-launches as a fast spike across the net. You literally cannot solo it — the ball will always arc away from you to your partner, so every point requires a bump, a set, and a spike between the two of you. Positioning and communication are everything.
winRules: First team to 15 points wins the set. A point is scored when the opposing side fails to return the ball (team scores if the AI misses; AI scores if the team misses or lets the ball hit the sand on their side). Only a fully completed 3-hit sequence can score for the team — a rally where fewer than 3 hits happen before crossing the net doesn't count.
moveRules: Player 1: WASD — free 2D movement anywhere on the near court. Player 2: Arrow keys — free 2D movement anywhere on the near court. Players can pass through each other (no collisions) so positioning is never blocked. No explicit 'hit' button — contact with the ball counts as a hit. The game tracks hit count automatically (1, 2, 3) and resets it whenever the ball crosses the net. On hits 1 and 2 the ball arcs in a gentle parabola toward the OTHER teammate (whichever player did NOT just touch it), forcing alternation. On hit 3 the ball auto-spikes fast and low across the net at the AI side.
setupInstructions: Horizontal court with a vertical net down the middle. Both players share the entire near court — no fixed zones, no split. Single AI opponent on the far side that simply tracks the ball and returns it Pong-style (no volleyball rules for the AI). Ball serves from the AI side at round start. Score displayed at top of screen. Current hit count shown near the ball as a small 1/2/3 indicator. Each player rendered in a distinct color so they can tell themselves apart in the shared space.
artStyleNotes: Sunset beach vibe. Sandy court with faint lane lines, ocean horizon, palm tree silhouettes on either side, a setting sun behind the net. Warm palette: cream sand, coral pink sky, teal water, deep orange sun. Ball is a pixel-art volleyball with subtle shading. Chunky 16x16 pixel art. Scanlines optional. UI in a retro SNES sports-game font.

name: Rhythm Relay
coopSpin: Note lanes are split between players — Player 1 handles left two lanes, Player 2 handles right two lanes. Some special 'bridge notes' span the middle and either player can hit them, requiring awareness of who's free.
winRules: Complete the song with combined accuracy above 80% to pass. Letter grade based on total percentage. Three misses in a row by the team ends the song early.
moveRules: Player 1: A/S keys for their two lanes. Player 2: K/L keys for their two lanes. Bridge notes in center can be hit by either player using their inner key (S or K).
setupInstructions: Select from 3 songs of increasing difficulty. 4-lane highway with notes scrolling down. BPM and note patterns pre-mapped to royalty-free music.
artStyleNotes: Vibrant disco/club aesthetic with rainbow gradients and particle bursts on hits. Dark purple background. Notes are glowing diamonds. Screen shake on perfect hits.
